title: New Investigation
description: Start a new investigation by defining your research question, selecting a data source, and configuring your initial workspace. Investigations structure your entire analytical workflow from hypothesis to findings.
tips: Write a clear research question before proceeding — it will guide your cohort definitions and analysis choices.
Select the data source that best matches your population of interest (e.g., SynPUF for testing, Acumenus for production).
You can always add more data sources and analyses after the investigation is created.
Tag your investigation with keywords to make it discoverable by colleagues.
